mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that suppresses household income and consumer spending, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and driving downward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized recovery in consumer and business demand, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52 percent over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average during an early-cycle expansion reflects accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, triggering short-term positive market momentum as investors price in rising profits.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of occurrences, or Rank IC drops below zero across consecutive monthly evaluations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion that supports broad corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters where GDP growth exceeds the threshold, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent forces market participants to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the negative correlation between CPI spikes and equity returns breaks down (correlation > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and wage growth, driving positive monthly equity returns through higher expected earnings.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline by more than 0.5 percent monthly, or interaction effects vanish when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million annualized during a late-cycle housing correction signals collapsing construction demand and financial sector stress, triggering negative short-term market returns as risk premiums widen.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the housing threshold loses predictive power when tested against alternative macro regimes.
